data.title: Federal Signal Raises Dividend to 14c Vs. 12c >FSS
Corn and Soybeans Under Pressure From Wet Forecast — Market Talk
data.description: Corn and soybean futures on the CBOT are down, as the weather outlook for the U.S. Corn Belt looks supportive for crops being planted this spring. Precipitation over the next two weeks is expected to be above average, says the Climate Prediction Center in its latest data. Analysts and traders believe the moisture will be beneficial for farmers when they plant crops. With traders reconsidering their outlook for the number of acres planted this spring, the weather forecast is a pressure point--corn is down 1.2% and soybeans are off 0.6%. Wheat is up 0.7% on Russia's export quota.(kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

data.title: Asking $88 Million: A Guggenheim-Inspired Miami Beach Mansion — WSJ
data.description:
By E.B. Solomont

Be the first to know about the biggest and best luxury home sales and listings by signing up for our Mansion Deals email alert.

In Miami Beach, Fla., a waterfront estate asking $88 million has elements inspired by Manhattan's Guggenheim Museum.

Located on La Gorce Island, the estate known as "Okto" is owned by the Kollmann family, wealthy Germans who own a conglomerate of textile companies. The Kollmanns bought the roughly 1.1-acre property for $15.7 million in 2010 from the property's developer, Todd Michael Glaser, according to property records.

The main residence is about 16,770 square feet, and nine bedrooms are split between the main house, a separate guesthouse and staff quarters, said listing agent Nelson Gonzalez of Berkshire Hathaway HomeServices EWM Realty. The property has about 260 feet of water frontage, with two docks, a 100-foot pool and a man-made strip of sandy beach between the house and sea wall, he said.

The Kollmanns declined to comment. They live primarily in Europe but have used the property as a vacation home over the years, enjoying boating and cheering on the Miami Heat basketball team, according to Barry Lapides, an attorney for the family. In general, they like to maintain a low profile. "They value their privacy," he said.

The house has a sculptural staircase and round skylight inspired by the Guggenheim, Gonzalez said. Other elements of the property have a tropical aesthetic, such as ipe wood and a coral keystone roof deck. Visitors drive through a Podocarpus hedge into a circular, pebbled driveway. The living room is 100 feet long.

The Kollmanns have made updates to the property over the years, replacing the floors, installing a new roof and upgrading the landscaping, Gonzalez said. They briefly listed the house for $38 million in December 2020, thinking they would not be able to use it because of the pandemic. "They quickly changed their mind when they saw Florida was open for business," he said.

The Kollmanns, who have homes in Germany, the South of France, Germany and elsewhere in Europe, have decided to sell the Miami Beach home because they are not using it as much as they used to, Gonzalez said. They also feel it is a good time to sell, given the strong dollar and massive price appreciation of Miami real estate since Covid, according to Gonzalez. "There's nothing else like this," he said. "Just the land by itself here is $48 million."

Entrepreneur Anand Khubani recently paid $100 million for three contiguous properties on La Gorce Island. In 2024, soccer star David Beckham and his wife, fashion designer Victoria Beckham, paid $72.25 million for a newly-built mansion on North Bay Road in Miami Beach. And Glaser is in contract to pay $105 million for a property on North Bay Road, about a half-mile from the La Gorce estate.

Write to E.B. Solomont at eb.solomont@wsj.com

data.title: Mirum Pharmaceuticals' Lipid Storage Disease Treatment Gets FDA Approval
data.description:
Mirum Pharmaceuticals' treatment for cerebrotendinous xanthomatosis, a lipid storage disease, has received approval from the US Food and Drug Administration, the regulator said Friday.

Chenodiol, with the brand name Ctexli, is the first FDA-approved drug to treat the genetic metabolic disorder in adults. It lowers abnormal deposits of cholesterol metabolites believed to be responsible for clinical abnormalities in the disease, the FDA said.

The effectiveness of Ctexli was tested in a 24-week clinical trial where patients either received the drug or a placebo. Patients who took 250 milligrams of Ctexli three times a day had a significant drop in certain cholesterol byproducts that are unusually high in people with cerebrotendinous xanthomatosis, compared to those who received a placebo, the regulator added.

Shares of Mirum Pharmaceuticals were down more than 1% in recent trading.

data.title: REGIONS FINANCIAL CORP SEC 10-K Report
data.description:
Regions Financial Corporation, a prominent financial services company operating through Regions Bank, has released its 2024 Form 10-K report. The report provides a comprehensive overview of the company's financial performance, business operations, strategic initiatives, and the challenges it faces in the current economic environment. Regions Bank, an Alabama state-chartered commercial bank, maintains a significant presence in the South, Midwest, and Texas, with a network of 1,253 branch outlets and 2,011 ATMs as of December 31, 2024.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Income: Regions reported net income available to common shareholders of $1.8 billion or $1.93 per diluted share in 2024, compared to $2.0 billion or $2.11 per diluted share in 2023.
  • Net Income Per Share: The decrease in net income per share from $2.11 in 2023 to $1.93 in 2024 was primarily driven by higher funding costs and a decrease in net interest income.
  • Net Interest Income: Net interest income (taxable-equivalent basis) totaled $4.9 billion in 2024 compared to $5.4 billion in 2023, reflecting a 36 basis point decrease in net interest margin.
  • Provision for Credit Losses: The provision for credit losses totaled $487 million in 2024 compared to $553 million in 2023, driven primarily by asset quality normalization.
  • Non-Interest Income: Non-interest income improved slightly, totaling $2.3 billion in both 2024 and 2023, driven by increases in most categories, led by capital markets income.
  • Non-Interest Expense: Non-interest expense was $4.2 billion in 2024, a decrease from $4.4 billion in 2023, driven by declines in operational losses and FDIC insurance assessments.
  • Effective Tax Rate: Regions' effective tax rate was 19.6 percent in 2024 compared to 20.5 percent in 2023, due to lower pre-tax income and tax preferential items.

Business Highlights

  • Banking Operations: Regions Financial Corporation operates through Regions Bank, with a significant presence in the South, Midwest, and Texas. As of December 31, 2024, the bank operated 1,253 branch outlets and 2,011 ATMs.
  • Geographical Performance: The company has a strong presence in Florida, Tennessee, and Alabama, with 270, 195, and 185 branches respectively. The economic environment in these areas is expected to mirror broader U.S. trends, with continued population growth outpacing the national average.
  • Business Segments: Regions operates under three reportable segments: Corporate Bank, Consumer Bank, and Wealth Management. Each segment contributes to the company's diverse financial services offerings, including retail and mortgage banking, commercial banking, and wealth management.
  • Regulatory Environment: Regions is subject to extensive regulatory and supervisory frameworks, including the BHC Act and Federal Reserve regulations. The company anticipates continued regulatory scrutiny, particularly in light of recent U.S. depository institution failures and evolving regulatory policies.
  • Future Outlook: The company forecasts real GDP growth of 2.2% in 2025, with expectations of slower job growth and stable unemployment rates. Consumer spending is anticipated to align more closely with after-tax income growth, despite persistent inflation pressures.
  • Operational Challenges: The company faces headwinds in the manufacturing sector due to global economic uncertainties and policy changes. However, a more conducive regulatory environment could boost merger and acquisition activity in 2025.
  • Loan Portfolio: In 2024, total loans decreased by 1.7%, primarily due to a decline in the commercial portfolio. The allowance for credit losses was adjusted to reflect asset quality normalization and macroeconomic conditions.
  • Liquidity Management: As of December 31, 2024, Regions Bank had $7.8 billion in cash on deposit with the Federal Reserve, with a loan-to-deposit ratio of 76%. The company maintains significant borrowing capacity with the Federal Reserve and FHLB.
  • Human Capital: Regions emphasizes building the best team, with a focus on talent management, professional development, and competitive compensation. As of December 31, 2024, the company employed 19,644 full-time equivalent employees.

Strategic Initiatives

  • Capital Structure Enhancement: Regions focused on enhancing its capital structure by participating in supervisory stress testing conducted by the Federal Reserve, maintaining a standardized approach under Basel III Rules. The company also reclassified securities from available for sale to held to maturity to reduce volatility in Accumulated Other Comprehensive Income (AOCI) in preparation for expected regulatory changes.
  • Capital Management: Regions authorized the repurchase of up to $2.5 billion of its common stock, with approximately 34 million shares repurchased by the end of 2024, reducing shareholders' equity by $614 million. The Board extended the repurchase program through the fourth quarter of 2025. The company maintained a strong regulatory capital position with Tier 1 and Total capital ratios well above the minimum requirements, and its CET1 ratio was estimated at 10.80% as of December 31, 2024.
  • Future Outlook: Regions anticipates a more conducive regulatory environment in 2025, which could trigger increased merger and acquisition activity. The company plans to continue its strategic focus on optimizing its capital structure and enhancing shareholder value through disciplined capital management practices, including potential further stock repurchases and maintaining strong capital ratios.

Challenges and Risks

  • Market Risks: The company faces market risks such as adverse conditions in financial markets and economic conditions, which could affect demand for loans and other financial services. Fluctuations in market interest rates and the shape of the yield curve may also adversely affect performance.
  • Credit Risks: Potential for greater credit losses in loan portfolios than anticipated could materially affect earnings. The company is also exposed to risks from changes in the soundness of other financial institutions, which could impact its operations.
  • Liquidity Risks: Ineffective liquidity management could affect financial results. The company relies on the mortgage secondary market to manage risks, and any changes in this market could impact operations.
  • Technology Risks: The threat of cybersecurity incidents and the need to comply with evolving privacy and cybersecurity laws could increase costs and potential liabilities. The company must also continually adapt to technological changes to remain competitive.
  • Strategic Risks: Industry competition, particularly from fintechs and technology companies, could affect market share and profitability. The company's operations are concentrated in specific regions, making it vulnerable to regional economic downturns.
  • Operational Risks: Potential for fraud, reliance on third-party vendors, and environmental liabilities from foreclosed properties are significant. The company must also manage risks associated with new product introductions and changes to existing services.
  • Reputational Risks: Potential damage from ESG issues and negative public opinion could affect customer and employee retention.
  • Legal, Regulatory, and Compliance Risks: The company is subject to litigation, investigations, and regulatory changes that could impact operations. Compliance with numerous consumer protection laws is essential, and any failure could lead to penalties and sanctions.
  • Management Strategies: Management has identified several strategies to address these risks, including enhancing liquidity management practices, investing in technology to improve customer experience and operational efficiency, and strengthening cybersecurity measures. The company is also focused on maintaining strong capital and liquidity positions to navigate regulatory changes and economic uncertainties.
  • Interest Rate Risks: The company is exposed to interest rate risks, with profitability dependent on net interest income. Changes in interest rates and the yield curve can impact the value of assets and liabilities, affecting financial performance. The company employs interest rate hedging programs to manage these risks and maintain a neutral interest rate risk position.

SEC Filing: REGIONS FINANCIAL CORP [ RF ] - 10-K - Feb. 21, 2025

data.title: BOC's Macklem Opens Door to Cuts In Event of Hefty Trump Tariffs — Market Talk
data.description: Desjardins Group economist Royce Mendes says Bank of Canada Governor Tiff Macklem's speech confirms the BOC would cut rates should President Trump impose 25% tariffs on Canadian nonenergy imports, and 10% levy on Canadian energy products. Mendes cites one sentence in the speech, in which Macklem says that cuts could help smooth the adjustment to tariffs "provided the inflationary impact of tariffs is not too big." Mendes says this is in line with Desjardins' view that BOC would cut its policy rate by a half- to full-percentage point more than otherwise should hefty US tariffs materialize. In a Q&A session with a suburban Toronto audience after his speech, Macklem cautions BOC can't allow a "tariff problem to become an inflation problem." (paul.vieira@wsj.com; @paulvieira)

data.title: Apple Intelligence Arrives at visionOS in April
data.description:
Apple said Friday it will bring Apple Intelligence to Vision Pro in April, offering text-based features such as proofreading, rewriting, summarizing, and ChatGPT-powered composition.

Additionally, Image Playground and Genmoji will also be added to visionOS, along with features like Smart Reply and Create a Memory Movie, the company said.

Apple will utilize on-device processing for most tasks and Private Cloud Compute for larger requests without storing or sharing user data.

Spatial Gallery for photos and videos, along with a new iPhone app for Vision Pro will also be made available, according to the company.

data.title: Top Midday Decliners
data.description:
Bluebird Bio said Friday it agreed to be acquired by Carlyle Group (CG) managed funds and SK Capital Partners.

Under the terms of the deal, bluebird shareholders will receive $3.00 per share in cash and a contingent value of $6.84 per share, payable in cash if bluebird's current product portfolio earns $600 million in net sales in any trailing 12-month period before or ending on Dec. 31, 2027.

Shares slumped 41% following a surge in intraday trading volume to over 4.5 million from a daily average of about 392,000.

AXT reported a wider Q4 non-GAAP loss late Thursday compared with a year ago and sequentially. Wedbush adjusted AXT's price target to $4.50 from $5.50 while maintaining its outperform rating.

Shares of AXT sank 31%, with intraday trading volume catapulting to over 5.3 million versus a daily average of about 361,000.

Akamai Technologies has "limited" short-term growth opportunities, BofA Securities said in a note Friday, following the company's Q4 results overnight. BofA downgraded Akamai to neutral from buy and cut its price target to $100 from $125.

Shares fell 20%, with intraday trading volume surging to over 13.6 million versus a daily average of about 1.7 million.

data.title: USDJPY falls below the 50% midpoint
data.description:
USDJPY technicals

The USDJPY has moved below the 50% midpoint of the move up from the September 2024 low at 149.22. The low just reached 148.98. The price is also below a swing area between 149.078 and 149.39.

Sellers looking for more downside could now use the 149.39 as the close risk level. Stay below is more bearish.

The low from December 3at 148.637 is the next target on more selling.

data.title: Equity Markets Decline Intraday After Macro Data
data.description:
US benchmark equity indexes were lower intraday as markets analyzed the latest economic data, including a survey showing a slump in consumer sentiment.

The Nasdaq Composite was down 1.6% at 19,634.5 after midday Friday, while the Dow Jones Industrial Average slid 1.4% to 43,543.5. The S&P 500 lost 1.3% to 6,037.1. Barring consumer staples, all sectors were lower, led by consumer discretionary and industrials.

In economic news, US consumer sentiment tumbled 9.8% sequentially to 64.7 in February, while year-ahead inflation expectations soared, according to a survey by the University of Michigan.

The survey indicated "fears that tariff-induced price increases are imminent," Surveys of Consumers Director Joanne Hsu said.

Output growth in the US private sector unexpectedly slowed in February as the services sector contracted, while optimism waned amid worries over the impact of tariffs and domestic spending cuts, according to S&P Global's flash purchasing managers' index.

Existing home sales in the US dropped more than projected in January amid elevated mortgage rates and house prices, according to National Association of Realtors data.

"Mortgage rates have refused to budge for several months despite multiple rounds of short-term interest rate cuts by the Federal Reserve," NAR Chief Economist Lawrence Yun said. "When combined with elevated home prices, housing affordability remains a major challenge."

The 10-year US Treasury yield was down 7.9 basis points at 4.42% intraday, while the two-year rate lost 5.5 basis points to 4.21%.

In company news, Akamai Technologies shares sank 20%, the steepest decline on the S&P 500. The company late Thursday provided downbeat quarterly and full-year financial expectations.

UnitedHealth Group on Friday refuted claims by The Wall Street Journal that the US Department of Justice had launched an investigation into the health insurer's Medicare billing practices. The company's shares were down 7%, the worst Dow performer.

MercadoLibre shares jumped 7.5%, the top gainer on the Nasdaq. The company late Thursday delivered a quarterly beat.

West Texas Intermediate crude oil was down 2.5% at $70.66 a barrel intraday Friday.

Gold fell 0.1% to $2,953.80 per troy ounce, while silver fell 1.5% to $33 per ounce.

data.title: Sector Update: Consumer Stocks Mixed Friday Afternoon
data.description:
Consumer stocks were mixed Friday afternoon, with the Consumer Staples Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLP) increasing 0.8% and the Consumer Discretionary Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLY) falling 2.3%.

In corporate news, Celsius agreed to buy energy drink maker Alani Nutrition for $1.8 billion in cash and stock. Celsius shares jumped 29%.

Tesla is being approached by a Japanese group with a proposal for an investment in Nissan following the failure of its merger attempt with Honda (HMC), The Financial Times reported. Tesla shares were falling 3.8%.

Ford plans to recall over 240,000 cars in the US because of possible problems with their seat belts, Reuters reported. Ford shares were down 1%.

data.title: Sector Update: Tech Stocks Fall in Afternoon Trading
data.description:
Tech stocks were in the red Friday afternoon, with the Technology Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLK) falling 1.8% and the SPDR S&P Semiconductor ETF (XSD) dropping 2.8%.

The Philadelphia Semiconductor index retreated 2.8%.

In corporate news, Apple Chief Executive Tim Cook promised President Donald Trump to move the company's manufacturing to the US from Mexico, Trump told a gathering of governors Friday, according to a Bloomberg report. Apple shares were rising 0.7%.

Akamai Technologies has "limited" short-term growth opportunities, BofA Securities said in a note Friday, following the company's Q4 results overnight. BofA downgraded Akamai to neutral from buy and cut its price target to $100 from $125. Akamai shares dropped 20%.

The Department of Justice and the Securities and Exchange Commission are investigating a $32 million deal between CrowdStrike and Carahsoft to provide cybersecurity tools to the Internal Revenue Service, Bloomberg reported. CrowdStrike shares fell 4.9%.

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Steel tariffs will raise production costs in region
data.description:
Fed's Goolsbee is lurking around saying that:

  • Steel tariffs will raise production costs in the region.

He has spoken a few times this week. As a result, what he says will likely not impact the markets.

Yesterday, Goolsbee commented on inflation and economic conditions, stating that the upcoming PCE report is unlikely to be as sobering as the recent CPI numbers. He noted that the labor market appears stable, while credit conditions remain tight. Goolsbee expressed concern about potential supply shocks, emphasizing that while the 2018 tariffs had little impact on national inflation, larger tariff measures could have a more significant effect, comparable to COVID-era disruptions.

data.title: Federal Signal Corporation Increases Quarterly Dividend by 17%
data.description:
(18:45 GMT) *Federal Signal Corporation Increases Quarterly Dividend By 17% >FSS
(18:45 GMT) Press Release: Federal Signal Corporation Increases Quarterly Dividend by 17%

Federal Signal Corporation Increases Quarterly Dividend by 17%

PR Newswire

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., Feb. 21, 2025

DOWNERS GROVE, Ill., Feb. 21, 2025 /PRNewswire/ — The Board of Directors of Federal Signal Corporation declared a quarterly cash dividend of fourteen cents ($0.14) per share on its common stock. This represents an increase of $0.02 per share, or 17%, compared to the dividend declared in the prior quarter. The dividend is payable on March 27, 2025 to stockholders of record at the close of business on March 14, 2025.

About Federal Signal

Federal Signal Corporation builds and delivers equipment of unmatched quality that moves material, cleans infrastructure, and protects the communities where we work and live. Founded in 1901, Federal Signal is a leading global designer, manufacturer and supplier of products and total solutions that serve municipal, governmental, industrial and commercial customers. Headquartered in Downers Grove, Ill., with manufacturing facilities worldwide, the Company operates two groups: Environmental Solutions and Safety and Security Systems. For more information on Federal Signal, visit: https://www.federalsignal.com.

View original content:https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/federal-signal-corporation-increases-quarterly-dividend-by-17-302382509.html

SOURCE Federal Signal Corporation

/CONTACT: Ian Hudson +1-630-954-2000, ihudson@federalsignal.com

EARLY WARNING REPORT ISSUED PURSUANT TO NATIONAL INSTRUMENT 62-103 - ACQUISITION OF SECURITIES OF MERIDIAN MINING UK SOCIETAS
data.description:
EARLY WARNING REPORT ISSUED PURSUANT TO NATIONAL INSTRUMENT 62-103 - ACQUISITION OF SECURITIES OF MERIDIAN MINING UK SOCIETAS

Canada NewsWire

VANCOUVER, BC, Feb. 21, 2025

TSX: MNO

VANCOUVER, BC, Feb. 21, 2025 /CNW/ - This news release is related to the common shares of Meridian Mining UK Societas (the "Issuer"). Ithaki Limited (the "Acquiror") announces that it acquired 25,641,025 common shares (the "Common Shares") of the Issuer at a price of $0.39 per Common Share for a total subscription price of $9,999,999.75 through a non-brokered private placement (the "Acquisition").

Immediately prior to the Acquisition, the Acquiror owned and/or had control over an aggregate of 14,285,000 Common Shares, representing approximately 4.7% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares of the Issuer.

Following completion of the Acquisition, the Acquiror had beneficial ownership and control and direction over an aggregate of 39,926,025 Common Shares, representing approximately 11.4% of the issued and outstanding Common Shares of the Issuer.

The Acquiror acquired the Common Shares for investment purposes. The Acquiror may, depending on market and other conditions, increase or decrease its ownership of the Issuer's securities, whether in the open market, by privately negotiated agreements or otherwise, subject to a number of factors, including general market conditions and other available investment and business opportunities.

National Instrument 62-103 also requires a report to be filed with regulatory authorities in each of the jurisdictions containing additional information with respect to the foregoing matters (the "Early Warning Report"). A copy of the Early Warning Report will appear with the Issuer's documents under the Issuer's profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca.

For further information, or to obtain a copy of the Early Warning Report filed under applicable securities laws, please contact Kristie Potts, director of the Acquiror at Ithaki@global-ags.com.

SOURCE Ithaki Limited

View original content: http://www.newswire.ca/en/releases/archive/February2025/21/c5795.html

Copyright CNW Group 2025

data.title: Sector Update: Health Care Stocks Fall Friday Afternoon
data.description:
Health care stocks dropped Friday afternoon with the NYSE Health Care Index easing 0.1% and the Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLV) down 0.4%.

The iShares Biotechnology ETF (IBB) shed 0.3%.

In corporate news, Novo Nordisk resolved its shortage of weight-loss drugs Ozempic and Wegovy, according to an update on the US Food and Drug Administration website. Companies such as Hims & Hers Health will no longer be legally allowed to make and sell compounded alternatives. Novo Nordisk shares jumped 5%, while Hims & Hers tumbled 23%.

Bluebird bio shares plunged 41% after the company agreed to be acquired by funds managed by Carlyle (CG) and SK Capital Partners.

UnitedHealth Group on Friday refuted a report by The Wall Street Journal that the US Department of Justice started an investigation into the health insurer's Medicare billing practices. UnitedHealth shares fell 7%.

Sector Update: Energy Stocks Fall in Friday Afternoon Trading
data.description:
Energy stocks were lower Friday afternoon, with the NYSE Energy Sector Index falling 0.9% and the Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLE) dropping 1.7%.

The Philadelphia Oil Service Sector index slumped 3.2%, and the Dow Jones US Utilities index shed 0.2%.

Front-month West Texas Intermediate crude oil was declining 2.4% to $70.71 a barrel while the global benchmark Brent crude contract was dropping 2.3% to $74.74 a barrel. Henry Hub natural gas futures jumped 3.4% to $4.29 per 1 million BTU.

In corporate news, ConocoPhillips said it signed a deal to sell its interests in the Ursa and Europa Fields and Ursa Oil Pipeline Company to Shell's subsidiaries for $735 million. ConocoPhillips was falling 3%, and Shell was down 2.3%.

Diamondback Energy on Thursday said Travis Stice will step down as chief executive but remain as executive chairman through the company's 2026 annual meeting. Diamondback shares were down 3.3%.

Solaris Energy Infrastructure shares jumped 25% after it reported an increase in Q4 revenue, while adjusted earnings declined.

data.title: Sector Update: Financial Stocks Decline Friday Afternoon
data.description:
Financial stocks were decreasing in Friday afternoon trading, with the NYSE Financial Index and the Financial Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLF) each down 1%.

The Philadelphia Housing Index was dropping 2.6%, and the Real Estate Select Sector SPDR Fund (XLRE) was shedding 0.9%.

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) was declining 1.6% to $96,899, and the yield for 10-year US Treasuries was dropping 9 basis points to 4.42%.

In economic news, the University of Michigan consumer sentiment index was revised downward on Friday to 64.7 for February from 67.8 in the preliminary estimate, compared with expectations for no revision in a survey compiled by Bloomberg.

Output growth in the US private sector unexpectedly slowed in February as the services sector contracted, while optimism waned amid worries over the impact of tariffs and domestic spending cuts, S&P Global's (SPGI) flash purchasing managers' index showed. The composite output gauge fell to a 17-month low of 50.4 from 52.7 in January, compared with the 53.2 consensus in a survey compiled by Bloomberg.

In corporate news, Coinbase said Friday that the US Securities and Exchange Commission agreed in principle to dismiss its enforcement case against the cryptocurrency exchange. Coinbase shares were falling 5.1%.

Mastercard's settlement of a lawsuit over card fees was approved by the UK Competition Appeal Tribunal, Reuters reported. Mastercard shares were down 1.1%.

Arbor Realty Trust shares fell past 11% after the company reported lower Q4 distributable earnings and net interest income year over year.

data.title: U.S. Oil Rig Count Rises By Seven to 488 — Market Talk
data.description: The number of rigs drilling for oil in the U.S. increased by seven this week to 488, the most since the third week of September, oil services company Baker Hughes reports. Oil rigs were down by 15 from a year ago. Rig directed at natural gas fell by 2 to 99 and were down by 21 from a year ago. Natural gas company EQT this week cited efficiency gains and strong well performance in raising its production guidance for this year, as well as bringing forward plans to drop one fracking crew at the end of 1Q. The company's 35% year-on-year increase in completed feet per day in the second half of last year "reveals how producers can improve gas supply without adding rigs," Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics said in a note.(anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Price analysis 2/21: BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, DOGE, ADA, LINK, SUI, XLM
data.description:
The failure of the bears to sink Bitcoin below $93,000 attracted buyers who pushed the price near $99,500 on Feb. 21. The bulls will try to strengthen their position by pushing the price above the psychologically crucial $100,000 level. If they do that, Bitcoin may move toward its all-time high.

When the price settles inside a well-defined range, traders buy near the support and sell close to the resistance. Therefore, buyers may find it difficult to push the price above the all-time high of $109,588. 

Significant buying or selling pressure is needed to push the price out of the range, which usually requires a trigger. Until that happens, the price may continue its volatile random price action inside the range.

Although Bitcoin has been range-bound for several weeks, inflows have continued into spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds. A post on X by CryptoQuant’s head of research, Julio Moreno, highlighted ETF inflows of $4.3 billion in Q1 2025, compared to $4.8 billion in Q1 2024. However, the analyst added that in Bitcoin terms, the demand is down from 100,000 Bitcoin in 2024 to roughly 41,000 Bitcoin in 2025.

Could Bitcoin surpass the psychological $100,000 resistance, triggering a rally in altcoins? Let’s analyze the charts of the top 10 cryptocurrencies to find out.

Bitcoin price analysis

Bitcoin rose above the 20-day exponential moving average ($97,700) on Feb. 20, indicating that the bulls are trying to seize control.

The $100,000 level could act as a stiff barrier, but it is likely to be crossed. The pair could surge to $102,500 and thereafter to $106,500. Buyers are expected to face significant resistance in the $106,500 to $109,588 zone.

Time is running out for the bears. If they want to stall the upmove, they will have to quickly yank the price below $93,388. That could clear the path for a retest of the critical $90,000 support. The bulls are expected to defend the $90,000 level with all their might because a break below it will complete a bearish double-top pattern.

Ether price analysis

Buyers pushed Ether to the breakdown level of $2,850 on Feb. 21, but the long wick on the candlestick shows solid selling at higher levels.

If the price remains below the 20-day EMA ($2,777), the bears will again attempt to sink the pair below $2,500. If they can pull it off, the pair could plunge to $2,400 and subsequently to $2,300.

On the contrary, if the price rises above $2,850, the pair could reach the downtrend line. Sellers are expected to defend the downtrend line, but if the bulls prevail, the pair could rally toward $3,400.

XRP price analysis

The bulls pushed XRP above the 50-day SMA ($2.72) on Feb. 19, but the bears pulled the price lower on Feb. 20.

The flattish 20-day EMA ($2.64) and the RSI near the midpoint suggest the pair could remain inside the symmetrical triangle for a while. If the price breaks above the 50-day SMA, the pair could rise to the downtrend line. Conversely, a close below the 20-day EMA could sink the pair to the support line.

The next trending move is likely to begin on a break above or below the triangle. A break above the downtrend line could propel the pair to $3.40, while a slide below the support line may sink the pair to $1.90.

BNB price analysis

Buyers are trying to push BNB above the 50-day SMA ($668), indicating solid demand at lower levels.

The short-term advantage will tilt in favor of the bulls if they push and sustain the price above $686. The pair could climb to $745, which is expected to pose a substantial challenge to the bulls.

If the price turns down sharply from the 50-day SMA, it will signal selling on rallies. The bears will try to pull the price below $650. If they succeed, the pair could dip to $596 and then to $557. That brings the larger $460 to $745 range into play.

Solana price analysis

Buyers have pushed Solana above the breakdown level of $175, indicating the start of a relief rally.

The 20-day EMA ($192) is sloping down, and the RSI is in the negative zone, indicating that bears hold the edge. If the price turns down from the current level or the 20-day EMA, it will indicate selling on rallies. The bears will then attempt to sink the pair to $155.

Contrary to this assumption, a break and close above the 20-day EMA suggests that the markets rejected the break below $175. The pair could climb to the 50-day SMA ($210) and later to $220.

Dogecoin price analysis

Dogecoin has been stuck between the 20-day EMA ($0.27) and the support line of the descending channel pattern.

The downsloping 20-day EMA and the RSI in the negative territory indicate an advantage to sellers. The selling could accelerate on a break and close below the support line. There is support at $0.20, but that is likely to be broken. The pair could then slide to $0.15.

This negative view will be invalidated in the near term if the price turns up and breaks above the 20-day EMA. The pair could then rise to the 50-day SMA ($0.31), which is expected to behave as a strong barrier.

Cardano price analysis

Cardano (ADA) has been witnessing a tough battle between the bulls and the bears near the 20-day EMA ($0.80).

If buyers push and maintain the price above the 20-day EMA, the pair could rise toward the 50-day SMA ($0.90). That suggests the pair could extend its stay inside the descending channel pattern for some more time. Buyers will gain the upper hand after they propel the price above the resistance line.

On the downside, a break and close below $0.73 could sink the pair to the support line. The bulls are expected to aggressively defend the support line.

Chainlink price analysis

Chainlink (LINK) has risen to the breakdown level of $19.25, indicating that the bulls are attempting to start a recovery.

The downsloping moving averages and the RSI below 44 suggest that the bears are unlikely to give up easily. If the price turns down sharply from $19.25 and breaks below $17, it will signal that the bears remain in control. The pair could then fall to $15.40.

If buyers want to prevent the fall, they will have to swiftly push the price back above the $19.25 resistance. If they do that, it will suggest that the markets have rejected the breakdown. The pair may rally to the 50-day SMA ($21.49).

Sui price analysis

Sui (SUI) rebounded off the $2.86 support on Feb. 18, indicating that the bulls are trying to form a strong floor at the level.

If the price maintains above the 20-day EMA ($3.44), the pair could rise to $3.74. This is a crucial resistance to watch out for because a close above $3.74 will complete a short-term double-bottom pattern. There is resistance at the 50-day SMA ($4.05), but if the level is crossed, the pair could rally to the pattern target of $4.62.

Sellers will have to pull the price below the $2.86 support to start a deeper correction to $2.39 and later to $2.20.

Stellar price analysis

Stellar (XLM) is facing selling at the 20-day EMA ($0.34), signaling that the bears continue to sell on minor rallies. 

The $0.31 support is the critical level to watch out for on the downside. A break and close below $0.31 will complete a descending triangle pattern. The pair may then slump to $0.26 and, after that, to $0.20.

Contrarily, a break and close above the 20-day EMA suggests that the selling pressure is reducing. The pair may rise to the 50-day SMA ($0.39) and later to the downtrend line. Sellers are expected to fiercely defend the downtrend line.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: Lam Research Insider Sold Shares Worth $9,907,200, According to a Recent SEC Filing
data.description:
Seshasayee Varadarajan, Senior Vice President, on February 20, 2025, sold 110,080 shares in Lam Research for $9,907,200. Following the Form 4 filing with the SEC, Varadarajan has control over a total of 181,451 common shares of the company, with 179,084 shares held directly and 2,367 controlled indirectly.

SEC Filing:

www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/707549/000070754925000031/xslF345X05/wk-form4_1740162517.xml
